user.title: HomeKit Climate Plus
user.description:
Set up a new bridge. Pick a name, a free port for the HomeKit server, and the climate entities to expose.

After creation, Home Assistant will show a persistent notification with the Apple Home setup code.

data_description.name: Shown in Apple Home during pairing.
data_description.port: Any free TCP port. Stock HomeKit Bridge uses 21064; accessory-mode bridges climb from there. A default of 21065 is usually safe.
data_description.pin: Format XXX-XX-XXX. Leave blank to let pyhap generate one; fix it here if you want the PIN to stay the same across restarts.

init.title: Climate entities
init.description: Pick the climate entities this bridge should expose. Next you'll be able to tune each entity's swing / preset / linked-sensor settings. The bridge will reload automatically when you submit.

entity_options.title: Per-entity settings
entity_options.description:
Tune the {entity_count} climate entity/entities you selected. Each has four optional switches:

- **linked_swing_mode** — attach a HomeKit SwingMode control if the entity supports swing
- **linked_preset_modes** — expose each non-`none` preset as a separate Switch tile
- **linked_humidity_sensor** — link a sensor to show as humidity on the climate tile
- **linked_battery_sensor** — link a sensor to report battery level on the climate tile

Field names are prefixed with the entity ID so each entity's settings stay distinct. Leave sensor fields blank for no linking.
